name: Case Study - Max Dobres - LRPS | RPS Distinctions Panel

step.name: Why did you want to gain a distinction with the Royal Photographic Society (RPS)?
step.text: I wanted to improve my general photography and applying for the Licentiate with its clear criteria provided a structured approach and a goal to aim for.

step.name: Why did you choose Alan to mentor you for a distinction and had you worked with Alan before the RPS Mentoring Course?
step.text: I had not worked with Alan before and choose him from a list of candidates from Google. I was looking for someone who was well qualified and who was focused on the RPS distinction pathway. Alan’s claim to get all candidates through the Licentiate was reassuring.

step.name: Describe how you found the RPS Mentoring Classes.
step.text: I used a mixture of classes and individual mentoring. This enabled me to learn from the experience of others but also concentrate on my portfolio.

step.name: What distinction did you apply for and were you successful?
step.text: I applied for the Licentiate and after Alan’s mentoring my images sailed through the assessment.

step.name: What was your experience like of the RPS Assessment Day?
step.text: The Assessment Day was very successful. The judges gave me lots of positive feedback.

step.name: Did you seek independent advice, feedback or input from anywhere else before your panel assessment?
step.text: I used my local camera club, but it would not have been reasonable to expect the same time commitment I got from Alan

step.name: Having completed the course and Distinction Assessment, what advice would you like to share with others?
step.text: To be successful in the assessment you need to understand the RPS criteria in detail and have the advice of someone who knows what this means in practise

step.name: What was the hardest part of gaining a distinction with the RPS and why?
step.text: It was not hard as long as you listened to exactly what Alan said and put it into practice.
